steps.title: Solve for n to understand the equation
steps.content:
Starting with $3n + 5 = 17$, subtract 5: $3n = 12$, then divide by 3: $n = 4$.
The tree took 4 years to grow from 5 feet to 17 feet.

steps.title: Interpret the structure of the equation
steps.content:
The equation models the tree height over time. The 5 is the starting height. The term $3n$ adds height over $n$ years. Since height = starting height + (growth per year) × (years), the coefficient 3 must be growth per year.
Check: $17 - 5 = 12$ feet of growth over 4 years $= 3$ feet/year.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A: Years to triple its height — the tree tripled from 5 to 15 feet; that is not what 3 represents in the equation. Incorrect.
  • B: Average feet grown per year — 3 is the coefficient of $n$ (years), so it is the rate of growth in feet per year. That is the correct answer.
  • C: Height when the tree was 1 year old — substituting $n = 1$ gives $3(1) + 5 = 8$ feet, not 3. Incorrect.
  • D: Average years for similar trees to grow 17 feet — the 3 is not a time value; it is a growth rate. Incorrect.
